summary: Audited the downstream knowledge pipeline from processed meetings. Mapped 11 potential destinations (6 active/partial, 5 missing). Identified 7 breakpoints: no post-archive pipeline stage (critical), no block quality scoring, one-way CRM feedback loop, Content Library integration is schema-only, B33/idea system partially wired, reflection pipeline does not exist, title quality constrains downstream. Documented 5 redesign implications including absorbing the stalled relationship-intelligence-os build (22% done, has usable promotion gate artifacts). Provided recommendations on 2 decision points: Content Library as primary knowledge hub, and deterministic vs LLM-driven zone assignments for each downstream step.
learnings: The relationship-intelligence-os build (stalled at 22%) designed exactly the post-processing pipeline that this build needs. D1.1 (contracts/schema) and D1.2 (promotion gate engine) are usable artifacts that should be absorbed rather than rebuilt.

brain.db has 7042 meeting_edges from a one-time backfill but relationship_edges, commitment_edges, and org_edges tables are all empty (0 rows) — confirming no live pipeline writes meeting intelligence to the graph.

Content Library v3 schema already has blocks/knowledge_refs/relationships tables designed for meeting block integration, but no code implements this flow. The content_ingest.py script has no meeting block content type.

The B32 position extractor is the ONLY working downstream consumer of meeting blocks — it runs daily at 5 AM and scans B32 files for worldview position candidates. B33 has no equivalent consumer despite being annotated 'feeds idea system'.

11 reflection prompt files (R00-R09 + RIX) exist in Prompts/Blocks/Reflection/ but have zero processing infrastructure — no scripts, no agents, no pipeline. This is a complete gap for the Pocket-to-reflection requirement.
